title: C3: Residue contract spec (the safe-mowing gate)
description: Define exactly what a closing epic must link before it can close: >=1 assertion-register entry (A*/I*/E*) or an architecture-doc edit. This gate is what makes deleting settled paradocs lossless. Single most important rule in the transformation.
design:
RESIDUE CONTRACT v0 — the gate that makes deleting a settled paradoc lossless.

PRINCIPLE (doctrine): "Partial work is allowed; lost learning is not."
This operationalizes the Parallax `learning state: retained/promoted` field as a
hard precondition on epic close + projection deletion.

TWO GATES before an epic may close:
  G1 (descent): V=0 — every child conjecture closed with a typed verdict, OR
      remaining children explicitly superseded/handed-off (edges named).
  G2 (residue): >=1 durable residue link that RESOLVES (see allowed sinks).

ALLOWED RESIDUE SINKS (priority order):
  1. assertion-register entry (A*/I*/E* in conjecture-assertion-ledger)  <- canonical harvest
  2. architecture/doctrine doc edit (SHA touching current-architecture.md, runtime-invariants.md, doctrine)
  3. incident/evidence doc (REQUIRED for falsified/weakened — the negative result)
  4. merged code commit/PR when the code itself IS the durable artifact

REQUIRED RESIDUE BY CLOSE TYPE:
  settled(supported):   sink #1 or #2 (or #4 if code is the artifact)
  falsified / weakened: sink #3 mandatory (Problem-Documentation-First; negative learning is most-lost)
  superseded:           successor epic linked via supersedes/discovered-from; open edges inherited
  blocked/open_handoff: NOT a close — epic stays open; open edges named

NOT VALID RESIDUE (anti-laundering):
  - the paradoc/projection itself (it is the scaffold being mowed; cannot be its own residue)
  - a ledger entry alone (process record != promoted learning)
  - "see the bead" / circular self-reference

MOWABILITY PREDICATE (what doccheck computes once C2 reader lands):
  a docs/mission-*.md projection is safe-to-delete IFF
    epic.status == closed  AND  all residue links resolve.
  doccheck emits a "mowable" list -> deletion becomes mechanical, not a judgment call.

ENFORCEMENT PATH: new doccheck rule R-residue, report-only first (per docs-truth philosophy),
graduates to a hard gate in C10.

OPEN POLICY CHOICES (need owner ratification — these are doctrine, not mechanics):
  Q1: Is sink #4 (merged code alone) sufficient for a SUPPORTED settle, or must every
      supported settle also leave a #1/#2 written-knowledge trace?
  Q2: For superseded epics, is a lineage edge alone enough, or must the successor also
      carry forward the predecessor's residue obligation until IT settles?
notes:
RATIFIED Q1 (owner): SUPPORTED settle accepts code-as-residue ONLY when the PR/commit is auto-linked to an assertion-register entry (>=1 line). Pure code with no assertion anchor does NOT close. Updated sink #4 accordingly.
PROPOSED Q2 (recommended, pending confirm): SUPERSEDED carries forward the predecessor's residue obligation to the successor (defer-but-never-escape). Escape valve: a genuine reframe discharges the inherited debt with a single assertion line naming why the predecessor frame was abandoned. doccheck R-residue walks the supersedes chain at close.
RATIFIED Q2 (owner): carry-forward confirmed. SUPERSEDED transfers residue obligation to successor (defer-but-never-escape); reframe discharges via single assertion line. Residue Contract v0 COMPLETE.
close_reason: supported: Residue Contract v0 ratified (Q1 code-needs-assertion-anchor; Q2 carry-forward). Deliverable = the contract in design notes; self-residue is the contract itself. Promote to doctrine in C10. Enables C9 mowability predicate.

title: C2: doccheck .beads JSONL reader (hermetic)
description: Add a pure-Go reader for .beads/issues.jsonl to cmd/doccheck so CI validates the beads store WITHOUT shelling out to bd. Keeps doccheck hermetic; bd stays an authoring-only tool.
notes: FINDING (carry to C5/R5): .beads/issues.jsonl export has NO top-level parent field; parentage = dependencies[] edge type 'parent-child' (issue_id=child, depends_on_id=epic). Reader falls back to this. Also: scanner buffer raised to 8MB (beads records carry long design/notes). KNOWN: readBeadsJSONL/beadsVariant flagged unused by linter — expected, additive; consumed when C5 wires import + C10 wires R5.
close_reason: supported: hermetic native beads reader landed; go test+vet green (independently verified). Receipt: commit a181f086. Note: code-as-residue assertion-anchor (Q1) deferred until assertion register is wired in C5/C10 — bootstrapping gap, tracked.

title: C1: Beads capability spike
description: Confirm beads supports the mapping: custom/labelled types (mission,conjecture,heresy), close-reasons or notes for verdicts (supported/weakened/falsified/superseded/discovered), append-only event log for the ledger, and clean merge under 12-way concurrent orchestration. Output: a can/cant sheet; flag any column needing a label-encoding fallback.
design:
CAN/CANT SHEET (spike findings):
[CAN] Custom types: -t conjecture accepted at create (label 'conjecture' also applied as belt-and-suspenders).
[CAN] Verdicts: close --reason carries free-text verdict (supported/weakened/falsified/superseded); pair with a label for queryability. 'discovered' = new sub-issue via discovered-from edge.
[CAN] Ledger: THREE encodings — --append-notes (in-place, simplest), comments (comment_count field), or first-class event beads (--type=event, queryable). Dolt history adds free per-write provenance.
[CAN] V-as-query: parent/child + blocked-by edges native; bd ready already computes the frontier (only C1 was ready).
[CAN] Sharing/merge: storage = embedded Dolt (cell-level merge + branching); .beads/issues.jsonl is the git-committed interchange (now auto-exported). 12-way concurrent merge is Dolt's design point but UNVERIFIED under load -> defer the real stress test to C8.
[FALLBACK] none required; no column needed label-only encoding.
VERDICT: supported.
notes: PASS 1 / Move: probe / Verdict: supported — custom types accepted; jsonl export live (11 issues); close-reason carries verdict text; event beads + dolt history available for ledger. ΔV expected -1.
close_reason: supported: beads natively supports every column of the mapping (types, verdicts, ledger, V-as-query, Dolt merge). No fallbacks needed. Open edge: 12-way merge-under-load deferred to C8.

title: Beads-native mission state (Parallax v2)
description: Mission conjecture: If beads becomes the native store for mission state (epic=mission, sub-issue=conjecture), then mission supervisability advances and the doc-truth surface (mission-graph.yaml + paradoc state + .ledger.md) collapses to one store WITHOUT losing learning. Witness: the active mission system runs on beads; docs hold only upstream ideas and harvested learnings; doccheck validates one store instead of three registries.
design: Decompose paradoc: structured state -> beads epic/sub-issue fields; ledger -> issue event log; DAG -> beads deps. Docs keep idea-seeds + harvested assertions. mission-graph.yaml -> generated projection. doccheck reads .beads/issues.jsonl natively (pure Go, no bd binary in CI). This epic is the live adoption evidence Parallax sec5 asks for; it dogfoods the model on itself.
acceptance_criteria: Active missions authored as beads epics; settled-doc lawn mowed through the residue gate with zero lost learning; doccheck R5 verifies the beads store + binding instead of hand-typed YAML; Parallax SKILL + doctrine updated to native.

title: C9: Migrate active backlog + harvest-and-mow settled docs (P4)
description: Port active missions to beads; pass settled/superseded paradocs through the C3 residue gate then delete (not label-historical). Triage the ~165 mission docs by motion.

title: C8: Orchestrator on bd ready (P3)
description: Orchestrator dispatch frontier becomes 'bd ready'; verify the 12-worktree merge storm on mission-graph.yaml dissolves into JSONL/dolt append-merge.

title: C7: Conjecture-native descent (P3)
description: Descend to sub-issues for active missions: V-as-query, verdicts-as-close-reasons, ledger-as-event-log. This is Parallax design sec4 v2 made real.

title: C6: Flip source of truth (P2)
description: Beads becomes authoritative; mission-graph.yaml demotes to a generated projection; active epics also get generated docs/mission-*.md views for filesystem readability. New missions authored as bd epics.

title: C5: Shadow-mode import (P1, reversible)
description: bd import mission-graph.yaml -> epics 1:1 (no conjectures yet). doccheck validates beads ALONGSIDE the yaml, report-only. Beads derived-from-yaml; fully reversible.
notes: RESULT (independently verified): 106 nodes -> 105 epics created + choir-pfg folded (external-ref mg:beads-mission-state-v0); 62 closed (45 settled + 17 superseded); 53 blocked-by edges, no cycles; idempotent (2nd run delta=0). Active set = 44 (31 open + 13 in_progress), 9 ready. FINDINGS for downstream: (1) repo uses yaml.v2 not v3; (2) 'bd label add' arg order is <id> <label>; (3) the 62 closed nodes are the C9 harvest-and-mow candidate set; (4) 'bd list' hides closed by default -> doccheck/projection must query all-status explicitly.

title: C4: Status/verdict vocab mapping frozen
description: Freeze Choir<->beads mapping: planned/working/open_handoff/settled/superseded/blocked -> open/in_progress/blocked/closed + close-reason + labels; conjecture verdicts -> sub-issue close-reasons; discovered -> new sub-issue via discovered-from edge; V = count(open conjecture sub-issues).
design:
VOCAB FREEZE v0 — canonical Choir<->beads mapping. doccheck and the Parallax skill bind to THIS.

MISSION STATUS (epic):
  planned       -> open (no assignee)
  working       -> in_progress
  blocked       -> open + label 'blocked'  (reserve native 'blocked' status for hard tool-level blocks)
  open_handoff  -> in_progress + label 'handoff' + reassign owner
  settled       -> closed, reason prefix "settled: ..."
  superseded    -> closed, reason prefix "superseded: ...", + supersedes edge to successor

CONJECTURE VERDICT (sub-issue close): reason prefix is the verdict, lowercase:
  supported | weakened | falsified | superseded
  discovered -> NOT a close; create a NEW sub-issue with a discovered-from edge (raises V)

KIND (epic label):           spine | side | docs_truth | evidence | superseded
MUTATION CLASS (label):      mc:green | mc:yellow | mc:orange | mc:red | mc:black
HERESY DELTA (label):        heresy:discovered | heresy:introduced | heresy:repaired

VARIANT V:   count(children of epic where status != closed). Derived, never hand-typed.
            (C2 reader implements beadsVariant() as the canonical computation.)

WITNESS / RECEIPT:  "Receipt:" lines in notes for trace/artifact refs; --external-ref for commit SHA / PR / gh-N.
BUDGET:             label 'budget:granted=N' / tracked in notes; solvency = a query, not a field.

PARSE CONTRACT for doccheck (so labels are machine-readable):
  - prefixed labels use 'key:value' (mc:, heresy:, budget:); bare labels are sets (spine, conjecture).
  - close-reason verdict = first whitespace-delimited token before ':'.

DECISIONS:
  - Rich Choir vocab is preserved as close-reason-prefix + labels (no information loss vs the old YAML enum).
  - Native beads 'blocked' status reserved for tool/dependency blocks; mission 'blocked' is a label so it
    coexists with in_progress work. Revisit in C7 if it causes ambiguity.
close_reason: supported: vocab freeze v0 complete; full Choir<->beads mapping with no enum information loss. Binds C2 reader (label parse contract) and C5 import (status mapping). Promote to doctrine in C10.

title: C10: doccheck enforcement + doctrine rewrite (P5/P6)
description: Graduate new binding rules (residue gate, V-consistency, heresy-scan beads bodies) from report-only as allow-contexts settle. Rewrite AGENTS.md/agent-parallax-rules/parallax-design to native. Wire Dead-End-Escalation and Root-Cause-Clustering as bd queries.
